task_completion.description: Create the six required satellites in GMAT
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI Resource Tree showing six Spacecraft objects named exactly TS_1A, TS_1B, TS_2A, TS_2B, TS_3A, and TS_3B.

task_completion.description: Configure the satellites with the correct orbital parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Spacecraft properties window showing Keplerian elements with SMA around 7071.14 km, INC around 98.19 deg, and ECC near 0.001.

task_completion.description: Set up the Earth point mass and J2 propagator
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Propagator/Force Model configuration window showing Earth as the central body with J2 terms enabled (Degree/Order 2) or Point Mass selected.

task_completion.description: Propagate the constellation for one full orbital period
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT Mission Sequence showing a Propagate command configured for the 6 satellites with a stopping condition of 1 orbital period (e.g., ElapsedSecs or ElapsedDays matching the period), or a 3D graphics window showing the completed orbital tracks.

task_completion.description: Save the GMAT script to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal (ls command), or text editor showing the file 'walker_constellation.script' located in '~/GMAT_output/' (or '/home/ga/GMAT_output/').

task_completion.description: Produce the geometry verification report with required data
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing 'constellation_report.txt' containing the final SMA, ECC, INC, RAAN, and TA for all six satellites, the calculated orbital period in minutes, and the text '6/3/1'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used GMAT to model the constellation
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GMAT graphical user interface being actively used to configure the mission, rather than just writing a script blindly in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the simulation was actually executed
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the GMAT message window showing a successful run, output reports generated by GMAT, or the OrbitView showing the propagated satellite trajectories.

integrity.description: Verify the report contains calculated values, not placeholders
integrity.visual_evidence: The values for RAAN and TA in constellation_report.txt should be specific, calculated floating-point numbers reflecting a Walker-Delta 6/3/1 distribution, not generic placeholders or copied prompt text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target Semi-Major Axis (SMA) is 7071.14 km. The target Inclination (INC) is 98.19 degrees. The Eccentricity (ECC) must be less than or equal to 0.01. The GMAT script must be saved exactly at /home/ga/GMAT_output/walker_constellation.script and the report at /home/ga/GMAT_output/constellation_report.txt.
